what_it_does: Defines the typed Pydantic request/response schemas and the TOOLS registry for a fixed, generic MCP verb surface over node types, graphs, sources, runs, results, and surfaced decisions; also provides public verb-name aliases and a built-in self-check script.
observations: The file organizes verbs into fixed groups: introspection, sources, graphs/compositions, runs, results, and surfaced decisions.
Every verb has a params model and a result model; the TOOLS dict maps each verb name to a ToolSpec carrying both model classes, a summary, and safety annotations.
ToolAnnotations encode readonly, destructive, and idempotent flags and reject the incoherent combination of readonly + destructive.
Public verb aliases bind names like CreateNode to their params models, while result models are reached through ToolSpec.result_model.
A __main__ self-check validates generic instantiation, honest annotations, and the absence of per-node-type verbs.
SCHEMA_VER is a single module-level version for the whole tool surface rather than a field on every model.
standouts: The tool surface is a small fixed set of generic verbs; adding a node type is designed to add zero tools.
ToolAnnotations enforce that a verb is never both readonly and destructive.
Verb-name aliases point to params models, while result models live on the ToolSpec.
conventions: All schemas are Pydantic BaseModel subclasses with Field defaults for optional/additive fields.
Registry pattern: TOOLS dict keyed by PascalCase verb name.
Honest safety annotations on every registered verb for governance consumption.
Schema-additive evolution via optional params with defaults rather than new verbs.
Self-check assertions in __main__ exercise the contract invariants.
concerns: Verb aliases shadow the original class names (e.g., CreateNode = CreateNodeParams), which can confuse imports and type-checking.
Several mutating verbs (RunGraph, Retry, BranchRun, Feedback) leave annotations at default False rather than explicitly marking idempotency or destructiveness.
SetConfig and ResolveSurfaced are marked idempotent but not explicitly classified as mutating or destructive.
SCHEMA_VER is not attached to the registry or models, so callers must discover it separately.
notes: This is the contract layer for the MCP tool surface: generic verb schemas, a registry with safety annotations, and a built-in self-check.
questions: What code actually implements each verb's runtime behavior (the internal API the server calls)?
How are ToolAnnotations consumed by governance or the FastMCP server?
What is the intended behavior when RegisterSource is called with an already-registered id?
How does the server project these ToolSpec classes onto FastMCP tool definitions?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to define typed schemas and a fixed generic verb registry; the file does exactly that, plus aliases and a self-check.
intention_signals: It tries to make the agent/tool interface stable and small while remaining expressive across all node types, by separating the fixed verb kernel from the extensible type registry and by surfacing safety metadata for governance.
novelty: The explicit design rule that adding a node type adds zero tools, with type as a parameter rather than part of the verb name, and the built-in self-check that proves no per-type leakage.
contribution: A typed, self-describing MCP tool contract with a generic verb kernel, Pydantic schemas, a registry with safety annotations, and a self-check that guards against per-node-type verb proliferation.
summary_for_embedding: Typed Pydantic schemas and registry for a fixed generic MCP tool surface over node types, graphs, sources, runs, results, and surfaced decisions.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a stable, introspectable, safety-annotated verb contract that lets agents and canvas use the same small generic tool set across all node types.

symbols.does: Empty parameter model for the GetTypeGraph verb.
symbols.description: Represents the request shape for retrieving the entire type-graph; carries no fields because the operation is global and unconditional.

symbols.does: Result model holding types and extends edges.
symbols.description: Returns the list of NodeType records and a mapping from type name to parent type name representing the extends relation.

symbols.does: Parameter model that names a node type.
symbols.description: Used by the ListByType verb to request all node instances belonging to a specific NodeType.name.

symbols.does: Result model for listing nodes of a type.
symbols.description: Echoes the requested type and returns the matching list of NodeInstance records.
Accepts an optional node id or type name; when omitted the verb describes the whole system.
Returns a generic dictionary whose shape depends on the requested target; mirrors the UI's own view.

symbols.does: Parameter model carrying a free-text query.
symbols.description: Supplies the query string for the Search verb across types, nodes, sources, and runs.
Represents one hit with a kind, id, and short summary.
Echoes the query and returns the list of SearchHit results.

symbols.does: Reference model for a registered source.
symbols.description: Holds a source id, its spec dictionary, and a state string defaulting to registered.
Takes no arguments because the verb returns every registered source.
Returns the list of SourceRef records.

symbols.does: Parameter model for registering a source.
symbols.description: Carries a spec dictionary describing the new source to register.
Returns the newly created SourceRef.
Supplies the source id for a read-only survey of its contents.
Echoes the source id and returns a dictionary describing what the source contains.
Takes no arguments because the verb returns all compositions.
Holds a graph id, title, and node count summary.
Returns the list of GraphRef summaries.

symbols.does: Parameter model for creating a node.
symbols.description: Specifies the target graph, the generic node type name, and an optional config dictionary; type is a plain str parameter so any future type works.
Returns the created NodeInstance record.

symbols.does: Parameter model for connecting two nodes.
symbols.description: Identifies the graph and provides an Edge record describing the port-to-port connection.
Echoes the graph id and the Edge that was created.

symbols.does: Parameter model for updating node config.
symbols.description: Identifies a node by id and supplies a new config dictionary.
Returns the updated NodeInstance record.

symbols.does: Parameter model for deleting a node.
symbols.description: Identifies the node to delete by id.
Echoes the deleted node id and a boolean flag.

symbols.does: Parameter model for validating a graph.
symbols.description: Identifies the graph to validate by id.
Associates a message with an optional node id or Edge, describing a contract violation.
Returns the graph id, an overall ok flag, and the list of ValidationIssue records.

symbols.does: Reference model for a live run.
symbols.description: Holds a run id, the parent graph id, and a status string defaulting to running.

symbols.does: Parameter model for starting a run.
symbols.description: Identifies the graph to execute.
Returns a RunRef handle for watching and controlling the run.

symbols.does: Parameter model for watching a run.
symbols.description: Identifies the run to snapshot by id.
Returns the run id, overall status, and a per-node status map.

symbols.does: Parameter model for pausing a run.
symbols.description: Identifies the run to pause by id.
Returns the run id and a status set to paused.

symbols.does: Parameter model for retrying a run or node.
symbols.description: Identifies the run and optionally a single node; None means retry the failed frontier.
Returns a RunRef for the retry attempt.

symbols.does: Parameter model for branching a run.
symbols.description: Identifies the run to fork and supplies config overrides for the alternative branch.
Returns the new RunRef and the id of the run it was branched from.

symbols.does: Parameter model for changing run priority.
symbols.description: Identifies the run and the new priority level.
Echoes the run id and the new priority level.

symbols.does: Parameter model for fetching run results.
symbols.description: Identifies the run whose output addresses should be returned.
Returns a mapping from node id to output port to resolved address.

symbols.does: Parameter model for fetching a run trace.
symbols.description: Identifies the run whose execution trace is requested.

symbols.does: Single step in a run trace.
symbols.description: Records a node id, its status, and a detail dictionary for that step.
Returns the run id and the ordered list of TraceStep records.

symbols.does: Parameter model for submitting run feedback.
symbols.description: Identifies the run and provides a correction string to steer re-runs or learning.
Echoes the run id and an accepted flag defaulting to True.
Represents a non-blocking prompt with an id and a list of choices.
Takes no arguments because the verb returns all surfaced decisions awaiting resolution.
Returns the list of SurfacedItem records.

symbols.does: Parameter model for resolving a surfaced decision.
symbols.description: Identifies the surfaced item and the chosen option.
Echoes the item id, a resolved flag, and the selected choice.
Encodes readonly, destructive, and idempotent flags; model_post_init rejects the incoherent combination of readonly and destructive.
Raises ValueError if a tool is annotated as both readonly and destructive, preventing misleading governance metadata.

symbols.does: Registry entry describing a single verb.
symbols.description: Holds the verb name, its params and result model classes, a summary, and ToolAnnotations; model_post_init validates that params_model and result_model are BaseModel subclasses.
Raises TypeError if params_model or result_model is not a Pydantic BaseModel subclass, failing loud at registration time.
Constructs a ToolSpec from a verb name, params model, result model, summary, and annotation flags; used to populate the TOOLS registry.
